questions: What would be needed to add a walkthrough command to helix-cli?
Can the existing server API surface support a CLI walkthrough flow?

answers.answer: helix-cli needs: (1) a new top-level 'walkthrough' subcommand in src/index.ts following the comments/inspect pattern, (2) subcommands for listing available walkthroughs (by ticket/run) and displaying walkthrough content, (3) terminal rendering of step-by-step tour data — either formatted output or structured JSON suitable for piping to coding agents. The existing HTTP client (src/lib/http.ts) and HELIX_TICKET_ID env pattern (src/comments/list.ts) are directly reusable.
evidence: src/index.ts: 3 commands (login, inspect, comments), switch-based routing
src/comments/list.ts: --ticket flag with HELIX_TICKET_ID fallback pattern

answers.answer: Partially. The existing POST /walkthrough endpoint returns full walkthrough data including tour steps, and POST /walkthrough/files returns file contents. However: (1) the findRunOrThrow bug (server-side) would need to be fixed first; (2) there is no GET endpoint for discovering which runs have walkthroughs; (3) the POST method for read operations is semantically awkward for CLI consumption — a GET-based endpoint would be more natural. The walkthroughData JSONB stored in SandboxRun could also be served directly without re-generation.
evidence: Server routes/api.ts lines 189-190: POST /walkthrough and POST /walkthrough/files
SandboxRun.walkthroughData: pre-computed JSONB available for direct serving

statement: helix-cli has no walkthrough capability today but has the right structural patterns (commands, HTTP client, env-based ticket resolution) to support one. The CLI alternative proposed in the ticket is feasible — it would require a new walkthrough subcommand, minor server API additions (GET-based walkthrough retrieval, fix findRunOrThrow), and a decision on terminal rendering format (interactive steps vs JSON output for coding agents).
